name: Feel-Felt-Found
summary: An objection-handling technique that acknowledges how the buyer feels, normalizes it through others who felt the same way, and then reframes with what those buyers found.
whenToUseIt: Use Feel-Felt-Found when the buyer needs empathy and reassurance before they will fully consider the response, especially on risk, change, or confidence objections.
steps: Normalize it by showing that similar buyers felt the same way.
Share what those buyers found after evaluating or adopting the solution.
example: A buyer says implementation feels risky. The rep responds, 'That makes sense. A lot of teams felt that way at first. What they found was that a phased rollout made the change much easier than they expected.'
